title: The Deal Doesn't Sleep
tagline: A nine-figure deal three days from signing, and a competitor who just found out about it.

wake_up.text: Your phone has fourteen unread messages before your alarm even goes off. One of them, from your MD, just says: "Call me the second you're up."

choices.text: Call the MD back before you're even out of bed
Shower and gather yourself for two minutes first

market_open.text: Markets open in an hour. The deal team's group chat is already lighting up — a competing firm has apparently gotten wind of your client's acquisition target.

choices.text: Read the entire thread before responding to anyone
Skim for the one thing that actually matters and move

deal_room_prep.text: The deal room fills up. Your analyst hands you an updated model — the numbers still work, barely, if nothing changes in the next seventy-two hours.

choices.text: Start the morning call

competing_bid.text: Mid-call, your phone buzzes: a rival firm has put in a competing bid on your client's target, ten percent above your agreed price. The client is on the line, waiting for your read.

choices.text: Advise matching the bid aggressively, right now
Advise walking away rather than overpaying
Propose renegotiating structure instead of just raising price
Loop in the senior partner before advising anything

bid_outcome_match.text: The client matches the bid within the hour. It's expensive, and it's decisive — the rival firm backs off by lunchtime.

choices.text: Get back to closing details

bid_outcome_walk.text: The client, to your relief, agrees the price got ahead of the fundamentals. The deal quietly dies. Somewhere, that's the right long-term call — today it just feels like a loss.

choices.text: Debrief with the team

bid_outcome_renegotiate.text: A restructured earn-out clause satisfies both the seller's price expectations and your client's budget. It takes an extra hour of calls, and it holds.

choices.text: Finalize the new terms

bid_outcome_senior.text: The senior partner backs a middle-ground counter. It works, but the client noticed the hesitation before the answer came, and says so.

choices.text: Move forward with the counter

compliance_flag.text: Compliance flags a disclosure timing question on the deal — probably nothing, but it needs a real answer before the next filing.

choices.text: Stop and resolve it properly with compliance before continuing
Note it for later and keep the deal moving

junior_analyst.text: Your junior analyst hasn't left the building in two days and just made a modeling error that could have gone unnoticed until the signing.

choices.text: Sit with them and walk through the fix together
Just fix it yourself and move on

client_dinner.text: A client dinner that was scheduled weeks ago, now landing in the middle of the most stressful three days of the deal.

choices.text: Attend and actually be present, phone away
Attend but keep working the deal from your phone under the table

diligence_redflag.text: Late diligence turns up an undisclosed liability on the target company's books — small, but the kind of thing that needs to go in front of the client before signing, not after.

choices.text: Call the client tonight and disclose it immediately
Handle it quietly and mention it in tomorrow's briefing

all_nighter.text: Signing is scheduled for tomorrow morning. The documents need one more pass, and there's a real question of whether that happens tonight or at 5 AM.

choices.text: Push through and finish it tonight
Get a few hours of sleep and start early instead

board_call.text: A last board call before signing. One board member has late questions about the deal structure.

choices.text: Take the time to answer every question thoroughly
Reassure them briefly and keep to the signing schedule

closing_night.text: Signatures go on paper. Champagne appears in the deal room, the way it always does, whether the deal deserves it or not.

choices.text: Head to the closing dinner

end_shift.text: The deal is done, filed, and someone else's problem now. Tomorrow's calendar already has the next one on it.

choices.text: Go home and sleep properly, for once

random_moment.text: The deal room hums along for a few minutes before the next call — small things happen in the gaps, never quite the same way twice.

choices.text: A brief market dip rattles the room before steadying
Your analyst catches a small error before it matters
Someone runs out for real coffee

triumphant.title: Deal of the Quarter
triumphant.blurb: Clean structure, fully disclosed, client thrilled. This is the deal that gets cited in your next promotion conversation.

steady_hand.title: Solid Close
steady_hand.blurb: No fireworks, no scandals — just a well-run deal that closed exactly the way it was supposed to.

ordinary_day.title: Another One Signed
ordinary_day.blurb: The deal closed. Nobody's writing a case study about it, and that's fine. Tomorrow's deal is already staffed.

burned_out.title: Running on Fumes
burned_out.blurb: You closed it, but you can't remember the last full night's sleep. This pace has a cost, and the invoice is coming.

written_up.title: Compliance Review
written_up.blurb: Something about how a disclosure was handled is now in front of compliance. The deal closed. Whether it should have, the way it did, is an open question.
